title: AWS Lambda
tldr: AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that runs code in response to events and automatically manages the underlying compute resources.
Supported runtimes: Node.js, Python, Java, Go, Ruby, .NET, and custom runtimes via Lambda Layers / container images.
Event sources: API Gateway (HTTP requests), S3 (object create/delete), DynamoDB Streams, SQS/SNS, Kinesis, CloudWatch Events, and custom applications via SDK.
Key limits: 15 minute timeout, 10GB memory (max), 250MB deployment package (zip), 512MB /tmp storage, 1000 concurrent executions (soft limit, can be increased).
laymanDefinition: Lambda is like a vending machine for code. You load it with your code (sandwich ingredients), and when someone presses the right button (event trigger), it makes exactly what was requested. Each press runs fresh — no old sandwich leftovers (stateless). You never pay for the machine itself, just for each sandwich made (per-invocation billing). If nobody orders, the machine sits idle costing nothing.

deepDive.heading: Lambda Execution Model
deepDive.text: Lambda runs code in isolated containers. Cold start: first invocation after idle — Lambda downloads code, initializes runtime, runs initialization code (outside handler). Warm start: container reused, only handler code executes. Provisioned Concurrency: keep N containers warm to eliminate cold starts. SnapStart (Java): faster cold starts using VM snapshots. Execution environment lifecycle: Init (create container, load code) → Invoke (run handler) → Shutdown (freeze/terminate).

deepDive.heading: Event-Driven Architecture
deepDive.text: Lambda integrates natively with AWS services. Synchronous invocations: API Gateway, Cognito, Step Functions — caller waits for response. Asynchronous invocations: S3, SNS, CloudWatch Events — Lambda retries twice, DLQ on failure. Stream-based: DynamoDB Streams, Kinesis — Lambda polls the stream, processes batches. Event Filtering: filter which events trigger Lambda (e.g., only S3 PUT events with specific prefix).

deepDive.heading: Lambda Best Practices
deepDive.text: Keep functions stateless — use external storage (DynamoDB, S3) for state. Minimize deployment package size — only include necessary dependencies. Use environment variables for configuration. Implement proper error handling and DLQ for async invocations. Use AWS X-Ray for tracing. Set reserved concurrency to prevent one function from consuming all concurrency. Use Lambda Layers for shared dependencies across functions.

deepDive.heading: Lambda Security and Permissions
deepDive.text: Execution role (IAM): defines what Lambda can access (DynamoDB, S3, etc.). Resource-based policies: defines who/what can invoke the function. VPC configuration: Lambda runs in a VPC with an ENI (Elastic Network Interface) — adds cold start latency. Secrets: use AWS Secrets Manager or Parameter Store, never hardcode secrets. Lambda@Edge: run Lambda at CloudFront edge locations for low-latency global processing.

deepDive.heading: Lambda Cost Optimization
deepDive.text: Billing: per request + duration (rounded to nearest 1ms). Free tier: 1M requests/month, 400,000 GB-seconds. Cost factors: memory allocation (more memory = faster execution = less duration, but higher cost per GB-second), invocation frequency, and duration. Right-size memory: test different allocations — increasing memory often reduces duration. Use Graviton2 (ARM) for up to 20% better price-performance.

interviewAnswer: Lambda is the core of AWS serverless computing. Design functions to be stateless and single-purpose. Use environment variables for config. Implement proper error handling with DLQs. Monitor with CloudWatch and X-Ray. Right-size memory for cost optimization. Use Provisioned Concurrency for latency-sensitive applications. Lambda is best for event-driven, intermittent, and variable-load workloads.

interviewQuestions.question: What is AWS Lambda?
interviewQuestions.answer: A serverless compute service that runs code in response to events, automatically managing infrastructure.

interviewQuestions.question: What is a cold start?
interviewQuestions.answer: The delay when Lambda initializes a new container — loads code, initializes runtime, runs init code.

interviewQuestions.question: What is Provisioned Concurrency?
interviewQuestions.answer: Pre-warmed Lambda containers to eliminate cold starts for latency-sensitive applications.

interviewQuestions.question: What is the Lambda timeout limit?
interviewQuestions.answer: 15 minutes (900 seconds) maximum execution time per invocation.

interviewQuestions.question: What is the difference between synchronous and asynchronous invocation?
interviewQuestions.answer: Synchronous: caller waits for response. Asynchronous: Lambda queues the event, caller gets immediate acknowledgment.

interviewQuestions.question: What is an event source mapping?
interviewQuestions.answer: Configuration that connects a stream-based source (DynamoDB Streams, Kinesis) to a Lambda function.

interviewQuestions.question: What is a DLQ in Lambda?
interviewQuestions.answer: Dead Letter Queue — failed async invocations are sent to SQS or SNS for later processing.

interviewQuestions.question: How is Lambda billed?
interviewQuestions.answer: Per request + duration (rounded to nearest millisecond) — no charge when code is not running.

interviewQuestions.question: What is SnapStart?
interviewQuestions.answer: Lambda feature for Java that reduces cold start latency by caching VM snapshots.

interviewQuestions.question: What is Lambda@Edge?
interviewQuestions.answer: Running Lambda functions at CloudFront edge locations for low-latency global request processing.

codeExamples.title: Lambda Function with API Gateway
codeExamples.description: Lambda function integrated with API Gateway returning a JSON response with CORS headers.

codeExamples.title: S3 Event Trigger Configuration
codeExamples.description: S3 bucket notification that triggers Lambda when a .jpg file is uploaded to the bucket.

codeExamples.title: Lambda with DynamoDB Streams
codeExamples.description: Lambda function that processes DynamoDB Stream events and sends welcome emails on new user creation.

codeExamples.title: Lambda Environment Variables and Secrets
codeExamples.description: Lambda using Secrets Manager for database credentials with caching across warm starts.

codeExamples.title: Lambda Reserved Concurrency
codeExamples.code:
# Set reserved concurrency for a function
aws lambda put-function-concurrency \
  --function-name payment-processor \
  --reserved-concurrent-executions 10

# This limits payment-processor to max 10 concurrent executions
# Other functions can still use the remaining account concurrency

# To use provisioned concurrency:
aws lambda put-provisioned-concurrency-config \
  --function-name payment-processor \
  --qualifier "prod" \
  --provisioned-concurrent-executions 5

# Provisioned keeps 5 instances warm — no cold starts for prod alias
codeExamples.description: Lambda reserved and provisioned concurrency configuration for controlling function scaling and eliminating cold starts.

mcqQuestions.question: What is the maximum Lambda execution timeout?
mcqQuestions.explanation: Lambda functions can run for a maximum of 15 minutes (900 seconds).

mcqQuestions.question: What is a Lambda cold start?
options: Delay in initializing a new Lambda container
mcqQuestions.explanation: Cold start occurs when Lambda creates a new execution environment — initializing runtime and loading code.

mcqQuestions.question: How does Lambda billing work?
mcqQuestions.explanation: Lambda charges per request and per duration (rounded to nearest millisecond).

mcqQuestions.question: What is the purpose of a DLQ in Lambda?
options: Queue failed async invocations for later processing
mcqQuestions.explanation: Dead Letter Queue stores failed asynchronous invocations for later analysis or retry.

mcqQuestions.question: What does Provisioned Concurrency do?
options: Keeps containers warm to eliminate cold starts
mcqQuestions.explanation: Provisioned Concurrency pre-warms Lambda containers to eliminate cold starts.

mcqQuestions.question: Which Lambda invocation type is best for processing S3 events?
mcqQuestions.explanation: S3 events use asynchronous invocation — Lambda receives the event and processes it independently.
